seo.title: Litigation Firm AI Engagement: First Drafts + Practice Intelligence | Dodonai
seo.description: How a multi-attorney litigation firm used drafting agents and a partner dashboard to put litigation AI first drafts and practice intelligence on autopilot.

descriptor: Multi-attorney litigation firm, several practice groups on one stack
headline: A multi-attorney litigation firm put first drafts and practice intelligence on autopilot
summary: A foundation plus drafting agents and a partner dashboard: the shape of a larger, multi-wave build assembled module by module.

problem.heading: The starting state
paragraphs: Associates rebuilt the same motion and pleading skeletons on every matter, and precedent was scattered across drives and inboxes. The structure of the work was identical case to case; the hours spent re-assembling it were not.

At the partner level there was no single view of the business (revenue by practice area, attorney utilization, matter aging, lead-source ROI, or pipeline), so management ran on gut and end-of-month scrambles.

built.heading: What we built
built.intro: A modular, multi-wave build starting with a knowledge foundation, then drafting agents, then practice intelligence.

results.heading: What changed
items: Motions and pleadings draft from the firm's precedent library at roughly 70 to 80% of first-draft structure, citing only verified sources.
Discovery operations surface substantive issues and draft deficiency letters across practice groups.
The managing-partner dashboard rolls up revenue, utilization, matter aging, and lead-source ROI, and answers 'what's the status on Smith?' in plain language.
Associates shift from typing the skeleton to sharpening the argument.
Every filing is attorney-reviewed; the agents draft, the lawyers decide.
